# _docsmith_docstring_template.docsmith_docstring_template { #ammonyte._docsmith_docstring_template.docsmith_docstring_template }

```python
_docsmith_docstring_template.docsmith_docstring_template(
    param_one,
    param_two=None,
)
```

One-line summary of what this function does.

Longer description, if needed, goes here.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**param_one**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[type](`type`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   What this parameter is for.

<code>[**param_two**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[type](`type`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   What this parameter is for. Default is None.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[type](`type`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   What gets returned.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Add a fenced ```python block here with a runnable example. If it ends with
``plt.savefig('docs/reference/figures/<Name>_example.png', ...)``, running
``python scripts/make_doc_figures.py`` will turn it into a rendered figure
on this function's reference page — see docs/MAINTAINER_NOTES.md.
